# Decompiled Source Reference

This document describes the structure of the decompiled Android APK source at
`app/src/main/java/com/shadowborne_games/oathsworn/`. It is a read-only reference
generated by JADX; do not modify it.

## Directory layout

```
app/src/main/java/com/shadowborne_games/oathsworn/
  book/
    Book.java             Base class for all chapter data
    ChapterN.java         One file per chapter (N = 1-21); Chapter11_5.java for ch11.5
    Section.java          Immutable data holder for one narrative section
    Choice.java           One button/choice in a section
    TimeAdded.java        Wrapper: how much time this section adds to the time track
    ImageLinks.java       Wrapper: drawable resource IDs for images
    ImagePositions.java   Wrapper: sort-key positions for images on the plate
    LocationsAdded.java   Wrapper: location IDs this section reveals
    ShowLocations.java    Wrapper: boolean flag to show the location list
    IsLocation.java       Wrapper: boolean flag marking section as a named location
    ClearLocationsList.java  Wrapper: boolean flag to wipe all current locations
    RemoveSpecificLocations.java  Wrapper: location IDs this section hides
  gameTrackers/
    GameTrackers.java     Abstract base: state persistence, navigation, time triggers
    TimeTrackers.java     Extends GameTrackers; implements time arithmetic and timePop()
    GTN.java              One file per chapter (N = 1-21, GT11_5.java for ch11.5)
  mainCampaign/
    SharedPreferencesSingleton.java  Android SharedPreferences wrapper
```

---

## `book/` package

### `Book.java` - chapter data base class

All `ChapterN` classes extend `Book`. The constructor populates the fields below;
`Book` itself does not set them.

**Fields** (package-private, set by subclass constructors):

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `sections` | `Section[]` | Zero-indexed array of all sections. Index = section number used everywhere. |
| `timeList` | `int[]` | Destination section indices for all time triggers, e.g. `{116}` for ch13. The time VALUES at which those triggers fire are encoded in the corresponding `GTN.timeN()` method names, not here. Unused (empty or `{-1}`) in chapters with no time triggers. |
| `location` | `SparseIntArray` | Maps location ID (integer) to section index. Populated by calls like `this.location.put(902, 6)`. |
| `clue` | `int[]` | Two-element array of section indices that award clues. `-1` if chapter has no clue mechanic. |
| `clueLocation` | `int` | Location ID that unlocks when both clues are collected. `-1` if unused. |
| `clueLocationSectionNum` | `int` | Section index of the clue unlock target. `-1` if unused. |
| `deepwoodChapter` | `boolean` | True for deepwood exploration chapters (4, 10, 14, 17, 18). |
| `deepwoodTokens` | `List<Integer>` | Ordered list of deepwood-token section indices for this chapter. |
| `deepwoodMap` | `HashMap<Integer,Integer>` | Maps first-visit section index to second-visit section index. |
| `z` | `int` | Convenience sentinel: always `-1`. Used in place of literal `-1` in Section constructors. |
| `path` | `int` | Convenience sentinel: always `-1`. Unused but declared. |

**Key methods:**

```java
// Factory: returns the concrete Chapter instance for chapter number chapterNum.
// chapterNum 22 returns Chapter11_5 (chapter 11.5 is internally numbered 22).
Book getChapter(int chapterNum)

// Returns the Section object at zero-based index sectionNum.
Section getSection(int sectionNum)

// Looks up a location ID in the location map; returns its section index.
Integer getLocation(int locationId)

// Returns the timeList array: the destination section indices for all time triggers.
// The corresponding time VALUES are encoded in the GTN method names (e.g. time11()
// returns the section in timeList; the "11" is the time value, not stored here).
int[] getTimeList()

// Clue mechanic accessors.
int[] getClue()
int getClueLocation()
int getClueLocationSectionNum()

// Deepwood system accessors.
ArrayList<Integer> getDeepwoodTokens()
HashMap<Integer, Integer> getDeepwoodMap()
boolean isDeepwoodChapter()

// Returns [chapterTitleStringId, authorStringId] for the given chapter number.
int[] getChapterAndAuthor(int chapterNum)
```

---

### `ChapterN.java` - chapter data

Each chapter class has a single constructor that populates `sections[]`, `location`,
`timeList`, and the clue/deepwood fields.

**Asset name sentinels:**
- `this.z` (= `-1`) is used wherever a slot is unused.
- `R.string.X`, `R.raw.X`, `R.drawable.X` are Android resource IDs resolved at runtime.

**Example: `Chapter13.java` constructor (abridged)**

```java
public Chapter13() {
    this.sections = new Section[146];          // 146 sections, indices 0-145
    this.clue = new int[]{-1};                 // no clue mechanic
    this.timeList = new int[]{116};            // destination section(s) for all time triggers;
                                               //   116 = section to redirect to.
                                               //   The time VALUE (11) is NOT stored here --
                                               //   it comes from GT13.time11() returning 116.
    this.location.put(902, 6);                 // location 902 -> section 6
    this.location.put(903, 17);
    // ...
    this.sections[0] = new Section(/*...*/);
    this.sections[1] = new Section(/*...*/);
    // ...
}
```

---

### `Section.java` - section data holder

Immutable. All fields are `final` and accessed via getters.

**Constructor signature (9-choice variant, the maximum):**

```java
Section(
    int popUpText,       // R.string key or -1
    int popUpText2,      // R.string key or -1
    int popUpText3,      // R.string key or -1
    int popUpText4,      // R.string key or -1
    int audio,           // R.raw key or -1
    int audio2,          // R.raw key or -1
    int audio3,          // R.raw key or -1
    int audio4,          // R.raw key or -1
    int sectionText,     // R.string key or -1
    int sectionText2,    // R.string key or -1
    int sectionText3,    // R.string key or -1
    int sectionText4,    // R.string key or -1
    LocationsAdded locationsAdded,
    ShowLocations showLocations,
    IsLocation isLocation,
    ClearLocationsList clearLocationsList,
    RemoveSpecificLocations removeSpecificLocations,
    TimeAdded timeAdded,
    ImageLinks imageLinks,
    ImagePositions imagePositions,
    Choice choice1,      // omit trailing choices to use a shorter overload
    // ... up to choice9
)
```

The first 12 positional `int` arguments follow a strict order:
positions 1-4 are popup text slots, 5-8 are audio slots, 9-12 are section text slots.
All slots are independent; any can be `-1` (unused). The order within each group
(popup, audio, text) is the same, so `popUpText` corresponds to `audio` and
`sectionText` in terms of "content index 1."

**Overloads:** there are 10 overloads for 0-9 choices. The 0-choice constructor
(no `Choice` args) produces a display-only section with no navigation.

**Full field list:**

| Field | Getter | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `popUpText` | `getPopUpText()` | `int` | R.string resource ID for popup slot 1, or -1 |
| `popUpText2` | `getPopUpText2()` | `int` | Popup slot 2 |
| `popUpText3` | `getPopUpText3()` | `int` | Popup slot 3 |
| `popUpText4` | `getPopUpText4()` | `int` | Popup slot 4 |
| `audio` | `getAudio()` | `int` | R.raw resource ID for audio slot 1, or -1 |
| `audio2` | `getAudio2()` | `int` | Audio slot 2 |
| `audio3` | `getAudio3()` | `int` | Audio slot 3 |
| `audio4` | `getAudio4()` | `int` | Audio slot 4 |
| `sectionText` | `getSectionText()` | `int` | R.string resource ID for text slot 1, or -1 |
| `sectionText2` | `getSectionText2()` | `int` | Text slot 2 |
| `sectionText3` | `getSectionText3()` | `int` | Text slot 3 |
| `sectionText4` | `getSectionText4()` | `int` | Text slot 4 |
| `locationsAdded` | `getLocationsAdded()` | `LocationsAdded` | Location IDs to reveal |
| `showLocations` | `getShowLocations()` | `ShowLocations` | Show location list flag |
| `isLocation` | `getIsLocation()` | `IsLocation` | This section is a named location |
| `clearLocationsList` | `getClearLocationsList()` | `ClearLocationsList` | Wipe current locations |
| `removeSpecificLocations` | `getRemoveSpecificLocations()` | `RemoveSpecificLocations` | Location IDs to hide |
| `timeAdded` | `getTimeAdded()` | `TimeAdded` | Time added to track when leaving (-1 = no time) |
| `imageLinks` | `getImageLinks()` | `ImageLinks` | R.drawable IDs for images |
| `imagePositions` | `getImagePositions()` | `ImagePositions` | Sort-key positions for images |
| `choice1`-`choice9` | `getChoice1()`-`getChoice9()` | `Choice` | Null if fewer choices than the slot |

**Concrete example from `Chapter1.java`:**

```java
this.sections[57] = new Section(
    this.z,                                       // popUpText:  none
    this.z,                                       // popUpText2: none
    this.z,                                       // popUpText3: none
    this.z,                                       // popUpText4: none
    R.raw.chp1_21_3__a,                           // audio:  ch1 entry 21 subentry 3 slot A
    this.z,                                       // audio2: none
    this.z,                                       // audio3: none
    this.z,                                       // audio4: none
    R.string.chp1_21_3__a,                        // sectionText:  ch1 entry 21 subentry 3 slot A
    this.z,                                       // sectionText2: none
    this.z,                                       // sectionText3: none
    this.z,                                       // sectionText4: none
    new LocationsAdded(new int[]{-1}),            // no locations to add
    new ShowLocations(false),                     // don't show location list
    new IsLocation(false),                        // this section is not a location
    new ClearLocationsList(false),                // don't clear locations
    new RemoveSpecificLocations(new int[]{-1}),   // no locations to remove
    new TimeAdded(1),                             // adds 1 to the time track when leaving
    new ImageLinks(new int[]{-1}),               // no images
    new ImagePositions(new int[]{-1}),           // no image positions
    new Choice(R.string.btn1_21_3__a, 59),       // button 1: "choice text" -> section 59
    new Choice(R.string.btn1_21_3__b, 60)        // button 2: "choice text" -> section 60
);
```

---

### `Choice.java`

Simple pair of (button label string ID, destination section number).

```java
Choice(int choiceText, int nextSectionNum)
```

| Argument | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `choiceText` | `int` | R.string resource ID for the button label |
| `nextSectionNum` | `int` | Destination section index, or a sentinel value |

**Sentinel values for `nextSectionNum`:**

| Value | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `-1` | Chapter complete (end of chapter) |
| `-2` | Player died (restart chapter from beginning) |
| `-3` | Return to `nextPositionToken` (resume main story after a time-triggered detour) |
| any other int | Normal navigation: go to that section index |

---

### Helper wrapper classes

All are `final` with a single constructor and a getter. All wrap either a single
`int` (boolean wrapper) or an `int[]` (array wrapper).

| Class | Constructor | Getter | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `TimeAdded` | `TimeAdded(int timeAdded)` | `getTimeAdded()` | `-1` = no time; positive values accumulate |
| `ImageLinks` | `ImageLinks(int[] imageLinks)` | `getImageLinks()` | R.drawable IDs; `[-1]` = no images |
| `ImagePositions` | `ImagePositions(int[] imagePositions)` | `getImagePositions()` | Sort keys (e.g. `[4]`); `[-1]` = no images |
| `LocationsAdded` | `LocationsAdded(int[] locationsAdded)` | `getLocationsAdded()` | Location IDs to add; `[-1]` = none |
| `ShowLocations` | `ShowLocations(boolean showLocations)` | `getShowLocations()` | Show the location list after visiting |
| `IsLocation` | `IsLocation(boolean location)` | `getLocation()` | This section is itself a named location |
| `ClearLocationsList` | `ClearLocationsList(boolean clearLocationsList)` | `getClearLocationsList()` | Wipe all current locations |
| `RemoveSpecificLocations` | `RemoveSpecificLocations(int[] removeSpecificLocations)` | `getRemoveSpecificLocations()` | Location IDs to hide; `[-1]` = none |

---

## `gameTrackers/` package

### `GameTrackers.java` - abstract base

Manages all persistent game state via `SharedPreferencesSingleton`. All state is
stored per-chapter using the chapter number as a namespace key.

**Persistent state fields** (stored in SharedPreferences, loaded on demand):

| Key | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `sectionsList` | `ArrayList<Integer>` | Navigation history stack. Last element = current section. |
| `locationsList` | `ArrayList<Integer>` | Currently visible location IDs. |
| `timeAddedList` | `ArrayList<Integer>` | History of `timeAdded` values, one per section visited. Used for undo. |
| `removedLocationsList` | `ArrayList<Integer>` | Locations moved off the visible list; restored on back-navigate. |
| `amountErased` | `ArrayList<Integer>` | Stack of location-list sizes at each `clearLocationsList` call. Used for undo. |
| `nextPositionToken` | `int` | Last section number that was on the "normal path". Used as the return target for `-3` choices. |
| `nextPositionTokenWhenTimeWasTripped` | `ArrayList<Integer>` | Bookkeeping array for location undo when time trigger fires. |
| `timeTrackRedirectedSectionNum` | `int` | Section to jump to next time `setCurrentSectionNum` runs (-1 = no redirect pending). |
| `unvisitedDeepwoodTokens` | `ArrayList<Integer>` | Deepwood token section indices not yet visited (first-time visit). |

**Abstract methods** (each GTN class must implement):

```java
// Returns true if section i should update nextPositionToken when navigated to.
// Chapters with path-branching override this with range checks.
protected abstract boolean onNormalPath(int i);

// Returns the section index to redirect to when cumulative time equals N.
// Returns -1 if there is no trigger at this time value.
// TimeTrackers provides default implementations returning -1 for all.
public abstract int time1();
public abstract int time2();
// ... through ...
public abstract int time32();

// Core time-check method called after adding time. Returns [redirectSectionNum, remainingTime].
// redirectSectionNum = -1 if no trigger fired.
protected abstract int[] timePop(int chapterNum, int timeAdded);

// Add/remove/get/clear the time track for a chapter.
public abstract void addTime(int chapterNum, int timeAdded);
public abstract void removeTime(int chapterNum, int timeAdded);
public abstract int getTime(int chapterNum);
public abstract void clearAllChapterTime(int chapterNum);
```

**Key concrete methods:**

```java
// Constructor: obtains SharedPreferences singleton, sets chapterNum.
GameTrackers(Context context, int chapterNum)

// Called when navigating forward to a new section. Updates sectionsList, locations,
// clues, time-track redirect, and deepwood plate state. All parameters come from
// the Section object for the section being navigated FROM (or the prior state).
void setCurrentSectionNum(
    int chapterNum,
    int nextSectionNum,        // destination section index
    int[] locationsToAdd,      // from Section.getLocationsAdded()
    boolean clearLocations,    // from Section.getClearLocationsList()
    int[] locationsToRemove,   // from Section.getRemoveSpecificLocations()
    int[] clueArray,           // from Chapter.getClue()
    int clueLocationSectionNum // from Chapter.getClueLocationSectionNum()
)

// Called when navigating back. Undoes the effects of setCurrentSectionNum.
void removeCurrentSectionNum(
    int chapterNum,
    int[] locationsAdded,      // from the section being un-visited
    boolean isLocation,        // whether the section was a named location
    boolean clearLocations,
    int[] locationsToRemove,
    int clueLocationSectionNum,
    int[] clueArray,
    int clueLocation,
    int[] removeSpecificLocations,
    int numRemoved,
    GameTrackers gameTrackers  // self-reference, used for removeTime()
)

// Called after navigating forward. If timeAdded != -1: increments time track,
// conditionally updates nextPositionToken (if not -3 and onNormalPath is true),
// then calls timePop to check for a time-trigger redirect.
void manageTime(
    GameTrackers gameTrackers, // self-reference, used for addTime() and timePop()
    int timeAdded,             // from Section.getTimeAdded(); -1 = skip entirely
    int nextSectionNum         // the destination section number
)

// Returns nextPositionToken (the last "normal path" section). Called when the
// player hits a -3 choice to resume the main story after a time-triggered detour.
int returnToNextPositionToken(int chapterNum)

// Returns the current section number (last element of sectionsList), or 0 if empty.
int getCurrentSectionNum(int chapterNum)

// Resets all chapter state. Called when the player dies (-2 choice).
int diedRestartChapter(int chapterNum)  // always returns 0

// Deepwood helpers (only relevant for deepwood chapters: 4, 10, 14, 17, 18)
void setupDeepwood(ArrayList<Integer> tokenSectionIndices)
int secondDeepwoodPlateVisitChangesSectionNum(int currentSectionNum)
void reAddDeepwoodPlateIfPressingBackFromFirstDeepwoodPlate(int currentSectionNum)
```

---

### `TimeTrackers.java` - time arithmetic base

Extends `GameTrackers`. Provides implementations of `addTime`, `removeTime`,
`getTime`, `clearAllChapterTime`, and all `time1()`-`time32()` methods (default:
return -1). Also implements `timePop()`.

**`timePop()` algorithm:**

```java
// chapterNum: the chapter to check
// timeAdded: the time amount being added in this step
int[] timePop(int chapterNum, int timeAdded)
```

Behavior:
1. Gets current time value (after the addition) and walks backwards by `timeAdded` steps.
2. For each intermediate time value (from `previousTime+1` to `currentTime`),
   calls the corresponding `timeN()` method.
3. If any `timeN()` returns a section index (not -1), that is the redirect target.
4. Returns `[redirectSectionNum, remainingTimeAfterTrigger]`.
   If no trigger fired, returns `[-1, -1]`.

This means if a chapter moves from time 5 to time 8 in one step, it checks
`time6()`, `time7()`, and `time8()` in order and uses the first non-(-1) result.

---

### `GTN.java` - per-chapter game tracker

Each `GT` class extends `TimeTrackers` and overrides only the methods relevant to
that chapter.

**Minimal pattern** (chapters with no time triggers):

```java
public class GT1 extends TimeTrackers {
    private static final String TAG = "GT1";
    Chapter1 chapter1;

    @Override
    protected boolean onNormalPath(int i) { return true; }

    public GT1(Context context, int i) {
        super(context, i);
        this.chapter1 = new Chapter1();
        Log.d(TAG, "GT1: public Constructor calling super");
    }
    // No time trigger methods overridden; all inherit return -1 from TimeTrackers.
}
```

**Single time trigger** (e.g. `GT13`):

```java
public class GT13 extends TimeTrackers {
    Chapter13 chapter13;

    @Override
    protected boolean onNormalPath(int i) { return true; }

    @Override
    public int time11() {
        // When cumulative time reaches 11, redirect to section 116 (entry 13_30_1).
        return 116;
    }
}
```

**Calling pattern in `GT13`:**

```
time track = 10, player leaves a section with TimeAdded(1)
-> manageTime(gt13, 1, destinationSection) is called
   -> addTime(13, 1)          // time track becomes 11
   -> onNormalPath(dest) == true -> nextPositionToken = destinationSection
   -> timePop(13, 1)[0]
      -> walks time 11, calls time11() -> returns 116
      -> returns [116, 0]
   -> timeTrackRedirectedSectionNum = 116
-> setCurrentSectionNum(...) sees timeTrackRedirectedSectionNum=116 -> navigates there
```

**Path-conditional time trigger** (e.g. `GT7`):

```java
public class GT7 extends TimeTrackers {
    Chapter7 chapter7;

    @Override
    protected boolean onNormalPath(int i) { return true; }

    @Override
    public int time9() {
        // Returns section 71 (path B redirect target) if nextPositionToken is
        // in path B range [64, 143] or equals 145.
        // Returns -1 otherwise (path A: no redirect at time 9).
        // NOTE: JADX produced a bytecode dump for this method; see patches.js.
        throw new UnsupportedOperationException("Method not decompiled");
    }

    @Override
    public int time14() {
        // Returns section 63 (path A redirect target) if nextPositionToken <= 63
        // or equals 144.
        // Returns -1 otherwise (path B: no redirect at time 14).
        this.nextPositionToken = sharedPreferencesSingleton.getInt("nextPositionToken", this.chapterNum);
        int i = this.nextPositionToken;
        if (i > 63 && i != 144) { return -1; }
        return 63;
    }
}
```

---

## Asset naming conventions

Asset keys (string, audio, drawable) encode the storybook reference as:
`chpN_entry_subentry__slot` (strings/audio) or `chN_entry_subentry__slot` (drawables).

| Component | Meaning | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `N` | Chapter number (no leading zero) | `chp4_`, `ch13_` |
| Path suffix | `a` or `b` after chapter for two-path chapters | `chp7a_`, `chp7b_` |
| `entry` | Storybook entry number within the chapter | `_21_` |
| `subentry` | Sub-entry number | `_3_` |
| `slot` | Content slot: `a`, `b`, `c`, `d` (maps to array slots 1-4) | `__a`, `__c` |
| `p` prefix | Image page/panel (drawables only) | `__p1`, `__p4` |

**Examples:**

| Asset key | Decodes to |
|---|---|
| `chp4_21_3__a` | Chapter 4, entry 21, subentry 3, slot A (text/audio index 1) |
| `chp7b_12_20__a` | Chapter 7, **path B**, entry 12, subentry 20, slot A |
| `ch4_45_1__p1` | Chapter 4, entry 45, subentry 1, image panel 1 (drawable) |
| `chp13_28_20__a` | Chapter 13, entry 28, subentry 20, slot A (audio) |
| `btn13_30_1__a` | Chapter 13, entry 30, subentry 1, button A |

The `slot` letter (a, b, c, d) corresponds directly to the array index (1, 2, 3, 4)
within a section's text/audio/popup fields. `chp4_21_3__a` would be in
`sectionText` (slot 1), `chp4_21_3__c` in `sectionText3` (slot 3), etc.

---

## JADX decompile failure modes

Two classes of decompile failure affect this codebase. See `patches.js` for the
full explanation and all applied fixes.

**BUG A - bytecode dump in `onNormalPath()`:** GT4, GT10, GT14, GT17 produced Dalvik
bytecode dumps instead of Java for their `onNormalPath()` methods. The generator
cannot parse bytecode, so it defaults to `onNormalPath: "always"`. This causes
incorrect `nextPositionToken` updates in deepwood/two-path chapters.

**BUG B - missing or wrong `timeN()` return values:** GT2, GT5, GT7, GT9, GT15
have `time` methods that either throw `UnsupportedOperationException` (JADX gave
up entirely) or have the conditional guard extracted incorrectly (non-greedy regex
grabbed the first `return -1` instead of the redirect target). Both cause the
generator to emit wrong `timeTriggers` data.
